title: Subsurface valve
abstract: A subsurface valve including a valve body having an inlet, and outlet, and a valve portion. A plunger is shiftably mounted in the valve body to selectively fluidically connect the inlet and the outlet. A valve system is arranged in the valve portion. The valve system includes a fixed member comprising a magnet and a moveable member shiftably mounted in the valve body and mechanically connected to the plunger. A control system is operatively connected to the fixed member. The control system is operable to selectively adjust a magnetic flux of the magnet to shift the moveable member and the plunger.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A subsurface valve (58) comprising: a valve body (70) including an inlet (72), and outlet (74), and a valve portion (78); a plunger (112) shiftably mounted in the valve body (70) to selectively fluidically connect the inlet (72) and the outlet (74); a valve system (94) arranged in the valve portion (78), the valve system (94) including a fixed member (94) comprising a magnet (104, 106) and a moveable member (94) shiftably mounted in the valve body (70) and mechanically connected to the plunger (112); and a control system (23) operatively connected to the fixed member (94), the control system (23) being operable to selectively adjust a magnetic flux of the magnet (104, 106) to shift the moveable member (94) and the plunger (112). 2. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 1, wherein the moveable member (94) comprises another magnet. 3. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 2, wherein the magnet (104, 106) comprises a permanent magnet (104, 106). 4. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 3, wherein the another magnet (104, 106) comprises a permanent magnet (104, 106). 5. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 1, further comprising: a crossover fitting (124) arranged in the valve portion (78), the cross over fitting (124) including a recess receptive of the moveable member (94) and a passage receptive of the plunger (112). 6. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 1, further comprising: a conductor coil (140) extending about the fixed member (94). 7. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 6, further comprising: a connector (84) mounted to the valve body (70), the connector (84) being operatively connected to the conductor coil (140). 8. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 7, wherein the connector (84) comprises a tubing encapsulated cable (TEC) connector (85). 9. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 1, wherein the plunger (112) is formed from a material that is exposable to formation fluids. 10. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 9, wherein the fixed member (94) and the moveable member (94) are formed from a material that is distinct from the material that is exposable to formation fluids. 11. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 1, wherein the fixed member (94) and the moveable member (94) are fluidically isolated from the inlet (72) and the outlet (74). 12. A resource exploration and recovery system (10) comprising: a first system (14); a second system (18) including one or more tubulars (48) extending into a formation (36), the second system (18) being fluidically connected to the first system (14) through the one or more tubulars (48), at least one of the one or more tubulars (48) supports a tool (46); and a subsurface valve (58) fluidically connected to the tool (46), the subsurface valve (58) comprising: a valve body (70) including an inlet (72), and outlet (74), and a valve portion (78); a plunger (112) shiftably mounted in the valve body (70) to selectively fluidically connect the inlet (72) and the outlet (74); a valve system (94) arranged in the valve portion (78), the valve system (94) including a fixed member (94) comprising a magnet (104, 106) and a moveable member (94) shiftably mounted in the valve body (70) and mechanically connected to the plunger (112); and a control system (23) arranged at the first system (14) and operatively connected to the fixed member (94), the control system (23) being operable to selectively adjust a magnetic flux of the magnet (104, 106) to shift the moveable member (94) and the plunger (112). 13. The subsurface valve (58) according to claim 12, wherein the moveable member (94) comprises another magnet (104, 106). 14. A method of operating a subsurface valve (58) comprising: applying an application force to a magnet (104, 106) to shift a moveable member (94); shifting a plunger (112) operatively connected to the moveable member (94) relative to an inlet (72) and an outlet (74) to control fluid flow through the subsurface valve (58); and selectively adjusting the application force to control a magnetic flux of the magnet (104, 106) to control a force applied to the moveable member (94). 15. The method of claim 14, wherein applying the application force includes passing an activation current through a conductor coiled about the magnet (104, 106).
